title: Workflow Admin Application
content: A workflow administration tool I designed for organization administrators at FindLaw. It lets admins build custom linear workflows for processing member submissions — configuring each step with its own required data and review logic, from submission through to completion.

images.caption: Ideation and Sketching: Early-stage brainstorming using whiteboards, sticky notes, and marker sketching. Workshopping requirements with clients surfaces conflicting business needs and can resolve user pain points before committing to a design direction.

Structured Planning: A user story map I put together as the project moved from discovery into design. Identifying epics, personas, and user stories as a team is one of the most useful parts of the process — it surfaces alignment problems early.

Prototyping: Low-fidelity interactive prototypes in Balsamiq before moving to high-fidelity mockups. Lower fidelity keeps iteration fast and makes it easier to test ideas without getting attached to execution details.

Advanced Customization: The final application. One of the trickier design problems was avoiding an overly prescriptive data model — each workflow step needed its own configurable set of required fields. I built a wizard tool so any step type could be configured without building a separate editing experience for each one.
